$description: Ferrari — Italian motoring heritage as cinematic editorial. Near-black canvas (#181818, never pure black), single sans family (FerrariSans / Inter substitute) at modest weights (display 500, body 400, button 700), Rosso Corsa #DA291C used scarcely — primary CTAs, F1 race-position highlights only. Sharp 0px corners on every CTA, card and band: rectangular precision IS the brand. Cinematic full-bleed photography is the page chrome. Display headlines carry signature negative letter-spacing (-1.6px on display-mega, scaled). Derived from getdesign.md/ferrari (luxury automotive, chiaroscuro editorial). MIT.

accent.$description: Rosso Corsa — Ferrari's iconic racing red. Reserved for primary CTAs, the Cavallino mark, F1 race-position highlights. Used scarcely — never decorative.

accent-deep.$description: Rosso Corsa darker — documented for completeness; per the no-hover policy this is not used in preview output.

highlight.$description: Hypersail yellow — focus-ring + the Hypersail sailing program ONLY. NOT a general accent. Off-brand outside that scope.

black.$description: Body ink on Ferrari's near-black canvas reads pure white. Semantic-name kept, value flipped to invert the palette without rewriting layouts.

ink.$description: Display + body emphasis on dark canvas — pure white per Ferrari DESIGN.md.

chapter-slab.$description: Section divider band — paper-3 (slightly lifted from canvas).

graphite.$description: Body strong — same as ink. Ferrari has no second tier on dark.

steel.$description: Default running body text on dark — DESIGN.md `body`.

fog.$description: 1px hairline divider on dark — DESIGN.md `hairline`. Same hex as canvas-elevated; reads as a brightness-step.

fog-strong.$description: 1px hairline on light editorial bands — DESIGN.md `hairline-on-light`.

paper.$description: Canvas — Ferrari's near-black surface, slightly warm. NEVER pure black. The brand-defining cinema floor.

paper-2.$description: Canvas elevated — driver cards, livery photo plates (DESIGN.md `canvas-elevated` / `surface-card`).

paper-3.$description: Section divider band, slightly lifted from canvas.

off-white-2.$description: On-dark soft — secondary labels, footer body (DESIGN.md `body`).

rule-dark.$description: Hairlines on dark surfaces (alias of fog).

surface-dark.$description: Cinematic canvas — the dominant surface. Pure white display type sits on this.

surface-dark-elevated.$description: Driver / spec / preowned cards on dark canvas (DESIGN.md `canvas-elevated`).

surface-dark-soft.$description: Soft chapter-section dark band — barely lifted from canvas.

surface-cream-strong.$description: Strongest dark surface — selected tabs, emphasized bands.

canvas-light.$description: Editorial light band — preowned listings, pricing tables only. Used scarcely.

ink-on-light.$description: Default text on light editorial bands.

accent-teal.$description: Semantic info — info badges, callout backgrounds (DESIGN.md `semantic-info`).

white.$description: Canvas — Ferrari's near-black surface. Semantic-name kept, value flipped to invert the palette without rewriting layouts.

status-current.$description: Status: in progress — alias of severity-medium.

status-next.$description: Status: not yet started — muted neutral.

font-family.$description: Single sans family across every text role. FerrariSans is licensed; Inter at weight 500 with letter-spacing -1% is the documented open-source substitute. NO display/body family split — the whole system runs one sans.

font-weight.$description: Display 500 + body 400 + button 700. Ferrari's signature: editorial confidence at weight 500, never bombastic. Caption-uppercase uses 600 (one tier above body for label voice). Bold (700) is reserved for component titles and CTA labels.

medium.$description: DISPLAY weight — display-mega/xl/lg/md all run at 500. Editorial confidence, not bombast.

bold.$description: Button labels, component titles, race-position number-display.

font-size.$description: Sizes scaled to 1920x1080 deck baseline. Ferrari's web spec runs 11–80px (compact density); deck values scale up roughly 4x at the display end while preserving the 500/400/700 weight ladder.

slide-title.$description: Hero h1 / cover headline — DESIGN.md display-mega scaled. Weight 500, -1.6px tracking.

sub.$description: Sub-headline / section head — DESIGN.md display-lg. Weight 500, -0.36px tracking.

kpi-value.$description: Spec-cell number-display — weight 700, -1.6px tracking. Race-position highlights use the same geometry in Rosso Corsa.

kpi-key.$description: Spec-cell label — caption-uppercase. Weight 600, UPPERCASE, 0.1em tracking.

quote.$description: Pull quote — display-mega register. Weight 500, -1.6px tracking.

radius.$description: Sharp 0px corners on every CTA, card, band — luxury-automotive precision IS the brand. Pill (9999) reserved for badge labels only — that is the only place pill geometry is allowed. Form inputs allowed `sm` (4px). Ferrari falls in BMW's camp on radius (sharp), not Spotify's (pill).

btn.$description: CTA corner radius. Sharp IS the brand button — never rounded, never pill.

chip.$description: Badge-pill corner — the ONE place pill geometry is allowed (caption-uppercase badge labels only).

card.$description: Card / surface corner — sharp on every card and band.

padding-x.$description: Section padding `xxl` from the named 8px ladder — Ferrari editorial pacing.

layout.$description: Ferrari DESIGN.md layout policy — explicit 8px named spacing ladder. xxxs 4 / xxs 8 / xs 16 / sm 24 / md 32 / lg 48 / xl 64 / xxl 96 / super 128. Section padding is `xxl` (96px) for major bands; `super` (128px) reserved for hero band depth. Generous editorial pacing on dark cinema, tighter density on light editorial bands.

section-spacing.$description: Major editorial band rhythm — DESIGN.md `xxl` token.

card-padding.$description: Driver-card / preowned-listing internal padding — DESIGN.md `sm` token.

max-content-width.$description: Editorial body content cap — DESIGN.md max-width.

hairline-weight.$description: 1px divider on dark / light. Brightness-step + hairline carries the depth — no shadow tiers.

livery-band-pad.$description: Livery-band internal padding (DESIGN.md hero-band-light + cta-band-dark spec).

cover.$description: Ferrari cover treatment — full-bleed cinematic dark canvas, lower-left display-mega 500 -1.6px headline, single Rosso Corsa rectangular CTA + outline-on-dark CTA. The photograph IS the depth (when present); without a photograph, the dark canvas alone carries the cinematic register.

section-marker.$description: Livery band — full-width Rosso Corsa accent band, used at chapter dividers / major editorial breaks. Replaces BMW's M-stripe role and Spotify's equalizer-marker role. Per Ferrari DESIGN.md `livery-band` component: padding 96px, display-lg 500 -0.36px headline in white. Reserved meaning — never decoration.

fill.$description: Token reference — Rosso Corsa fill across the full band.

headline-size.$description: DESIGN.md display-lg = 36px web → `sub` token in deck.

photography.$description: Ferrari photography — full-bleed cinematic, edge-to-edge, no shadow. The photograph IS the depth treatment. Hero ratio cinema 21:9 / mobile 4:5; feature card 16:10. Headlines float over the bottom of the photo or sit in a tight band beneath. Studio plate when no photo present is the canvas-elevated #303030 surface.

headline-rule.$description: Display headlines run weight 500 (medium) — never bold. Negative letter-spacing on display IS a Ferrari signature: -0.02em on display-mega, scaling to 0 by body sizes. Body always 400 / regular. DESIGN.md is explicit: 'don't bold display copy — the cinematic photography does the visual heavy-lifting.'

tracking-em.$description: Default tracking on display sizes — Ferrari's editorial signature. Body stays at 0.

weight-display.$description: 500 — NEVER bold on display. Editorial, not bombastic.

chip-rule.$description: Caption-uppercase / button label voice. UPPERCASE, weight 700 on buttons (1.4px tracking @ 14px = ~0.1em), weight 600 on caption-uppercase (1.1px tracking @ 11px = ~0.1em). Both share the same ~0.1em tracking register.

shadow.$description: Ferrari elevation: photographic depth + brightness-step ONLY. NO drop-shadow tiers like Spotify. The single documented soft-drop is `0 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)` — used ONLY on hovered cards (not present in static deck output). The deck is shadow-free; depth comes from `surface-dark` vs `surface-dark-elevated` brightness-step + the cinematic photography itself.

elevated.$description: Cards on dark canvas use brightness-step (paper → paper-2), not a shadow.

hover.$description: DESIGN.md single shadow tier — hover only, off in static decks.
